description_long: This bungalow features a simple rectangular structure with a low-pitched, dark brown hip roof topped by a small golden finial. The exterior walls are light gray with subtle textural details, and the front facade includes a single door and two rectangular windows, arranged asymmetrically. The roof has several small, evenly spaced skylights or vents. The side and rear facades are more minimal, with fewer or no windows, emphasizing the compact, single-story design. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow architecture, and lacks any visible porch or veranda. The overall appearance is modest and functional, suitable for residential neighborhoods.
description: A compact, single-story bungalow with a dark hip roof, light gray walls, and minimal window openings. The design is simple and functional, with a small golden finial atop the roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door and two asymmetrically placed windows on a light gray facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the hip roof and two small, dark windows on the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wall with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the building's simplicity.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a partial glimpse of the front windows and door, and additional small windows on the side.
